summary: This is a brief meta-post where Joel Spolsky lists the 44 countries from which Joel on Software has subscribers. It serves as a milestone marker celebrating the blog's international reach relatively early in its history. There is no argument or analysis—it is purely a factual enumeration. The post implicitly signals pride in the blog's growing global audience.
key_points: Joel on Software had subscribers in 44 countries as of November 2000, demonstrating early global reach for a programming blog.
The list spans every inhabited continent, from Western Samoa to Iceland to Botswana.
The post functions as a brief community milestone rather than a substantive essay.
money_quotes: Joel on Software has subscribers in 44 countries.
key_insight: Even in 2000, a niche English-language software blog could attract a genuinely global readership across 44 countries.
